eval_set.note: held-out provenance set; single-judge content+format scoring via tools/eval_local_model.py

result.interpretation: The QLoRA adapter did NOT improve held-out CONTENT accuracy over the FP16 base: both score 23/32 = 71.9%. The adapter redistributed within domains (philosophy 6/9 -> 8/9; psychology 6/9 -> 5/9; religion content 5/6 -> 4/6) for a net-zero content change. The +6.2pt on the COMBINED (format AND content) channel is format-driven, not a content gain. The provenance gate left content unchanged at every rung (base == base+gate, adapter == adapter+gate).

claim: real-GPU LoRA eval-ladder datapoint (NOT a capability claim)
judge: single-judge content+format scoring (tools/eval_local_model.py); NOT the kappa>=0.40 / 2-judge-family / >=3-run / 95%-CI VALIDATED bar
honest_scope: One real-GPU QLoRA run (1 epoch, seed 0, 32-item held-out provenance eval, single judge). This is candidate-only / illustrative evidence that validates the GitHub-Actions -> RunPod -> eval-ladder path end-to-end and records an HONEST directional null: 1-epoch QLoRA on this curated set is capability-neutral on held-out content. It is NOT a validated capability claim and does not move canClaimAGI.

provenance.source: transcribed from the train-runpod step-7 eval-ladder report printed in the run log; the W2 promotion.public-report.json verdict is inside the run artifact
